summary: Solid Garden show with standout China>Rider jam and vibrant Althea.
blurb: The second set shines with a fiery China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition and an expansive Playing In The Band > Drums > Space > Not Fade Away > Stella Blue suite. Jerry delivers strong guitar work on Althea while his vocals show strain later in the night. Brent’s keyboards add fresh energy, though the mix is a bit dry.
review: On September 6, 1979 at Madison Square Garden the Dead delivered a performance that still feels fresh for anyone hearing it today. The first set was solid but uneven, with highlights like "Althea" where Jerry’s guitar sang and his voice held steady before later showing signs of fatigue on the encore. It’s in the second set that the night truly sparkles – the classic "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" jam roars to life, Brent Mydland’s keyboards weaving a bright new texture around the familiar groove. From there the band rides a wave through "Terrapin Station" into an epic "Playing In The Band" suite that blossoms into a long‑form drum and space excursion before resolving beautifully on "Stella Blue". While the recording is a good soundboard capture, it can feel a little dry and even suffers a brief power amp blow during the China/Rider jam, but those technical quirks never mask the band’s high energy and tight interplay. Fans looking for a quintessential late‑'70s Garden show will find this one rewarding, especially for its standout second‑set transitions and Brent’s fresh keyboard voice.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, praising the China>Rider jam, Althea, and the expansive second‑set suite, while the other two are mixed but not negative. The overall tone is moderately upbeat, warranting a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Best‑sounding soundboard source with clear capture of the band and the standout China>Rider jam, despite a dry mix.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider jam shines in the second set.
"Althea" showcases Jerry's crisp guitar work and strong vocals early on.
Terrapin Station leads into an expansive Playing In The Band > Drums > Space > Not Fade Away > Stella Blue suite.
Brent Mydland’s fresh keyboard energy lifts the jam sections, especially during China>Rider.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers strong guitar tones and an inspired solo on the China jam; vocal performance is solid early (Althea) but shows strain later, especially on Johnny B. Goode.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a steady bass foundation throughout; no specific criticisms noted in the recordings.
band_performance.Bob: Power amp blows during the China/Rider section but recovers quickly; appears a bit tired on later numbers like Sugar Magnolia and Johnny B. Goode.
band_performance.Brent: Adds energetic, bright keyboard interplay that enhances the jam sections; praised for fresh contribution despite some longing for Keith’s fuller sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum work is praised, with theatrical motorcycle revs adding flair during the drum segment; overall solid percussion performance.
band_performance.Billy: Alongside Mickey delivers strong drumming; part of the lengthy Drum > Space passages that some listeners found overly long but musically effective.
